text.title: embedding-models Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for embedding-models. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Sort batch response by item.index before mapping back to inputs; the OpenAI API may return embeddings out of order.
rule.rationale: Skipping the sort step produces misaligned embeddings for shuffled input batches, a silent correctness bug.

rule.statement: The dimensions parameter is only valid for text-embedding-3 models; applying it to ada-002 raises an error.
rule.rationale: ada-002 does not support Matryoshka dimension reduction; the parameter is silently rejected in some SDK versions and raises in others.

rule.statement: Mistral embed has a 512-token context limit on some versions; longer texts are silently truncated.
rule.rationale: Truncation without error means the embedding represents only the first 512 tokens. Pre-chunk long texts.

rule.statement: Always pass input_type to Cohere embed-v3; omitting it defaults to generic mode and degrades retrieval NDCG by ~5-10%.
rule.rationale: Cohere v3 models are trained with asymmetric input types. The generic default underperforms the asymmetric search_document/search_query split.

rule.statement: Instantiate SentenceTransformer once per process; do not create multiple instances on the same GPU.
rule.rationale: Each instance loads the full model into GPU RAM. Two bge-large-en-v1.5 instances on a 16 GB GPU will OOM.

rule.statement: BGE-M3 sparse embeddings are dict[token → weight], not dense arrays; adapt before storing in vector DBs that expect dense float arrays.
rule.rationale: Most vector DB clients (Qdrant, Chroma) expect float lists for dense vectors. The sparse dict format requires special sparse-vector handling.

rule.statement: Pin the exact model name (including version suffix if the provider uses them) in config — providers silently update model weights.
rule.rationale: Pinning guarantees reproducible results across ingestion runs. Unpinned models silently pick up provider updates, invalidating cached embeddings.

rule.statement: Run MTEB benchmark scores for your specific task type (retrieval vs. clustering vs. classification) — aggregate MTEB rank ≠ RAG retrieval rank.
rule.rationale: MTEB leaderboard aggregates results across 56 tasks. Your specific task (e.g., legal document retrieval) may have different winner than the overall leaderboard.

rule.statement: For OpenAI text-embedding-3 models, start with dimensions=512; benchmark vs. full 1536/3072 before paying for extra storage.
rule.rationale: Dimension reduction via Matryoshka is trained and lossless up to a point. 512 dims captures ~98% of semantic quality while reducing storage by 6.7x vs 3072 dims.

rule.statement: Always use Cohere input_type="search_document" at index time and input_type="search_query" at query time — asymmetric embedding is a significant quality lever.
rule.rationale: Asymmetric embeddings align the embedding space to the retrieval task, improving NDCG by 5-10% vs generic embedding.

rule.statement: Pre-normalize all embeddings to unit length before storing; dot product then equals cosine similarity and is faster in most vector DBs.
rule.rationale: Normalized vectors allow dot-product similarity to equal cosine similarity, which is faster to compute. Many vector DBs optimize for unit-normalized vectors.

rule.statement: Track the model name as metadata on every vector — if you ever need to migrate, you can identify which vectors need re-embedding.
rule.rationale: Multi-model indexes allow gradual migration and A/B testing. Single-model metadata on storage makes full-corpus re-embedding the only option.
